questions.text: What is one small thing you accomplished as a child or teenager that made you feel really proud at the time?
Was there a school project, sports moment, art piece or performance you were especially proud of?
What was the first time you felt you had really earned something through your own effort?
Did you ever surprise yourself by achieving something you didn't think you could do?
What compliment or recognition from someone else meant the most to you in your younger years?
Looking back, what early achievement still makes you smile when you think about it?
What was a bigger goal or milestone you worked hard toward in your 20s or 30s?
Was there a career, personal or creative accomplishment that felt like a major breakthrough?
Did you ever overcome a significant obstacle or doubt to reach something you're proud of?
What achievement made you feel you had truly proven something to yourself?
Was there a moment when you felt proud not just of what you did, but of who you became while doing it?
What accomplishment required the most persistence, sacrifice or courage from you?
Looking back, which proud moment do you now realise had a bigger impact on your life path than you knew at the time?
Is there an achievement you're proud of that other people might not fully understand or appreciate?
What do you hope your children, grandchildren or loved ones know about the things you're most proud of?
Was there a proud moment that came after a long period of struggle or failure — how did that feel?
What achievement do you look back on and think: 'I did that, and I'm really glad I did'?
If you could relive one proud moment exactly as it happened, which one would it be and why?
Looking at your entire life so far, what single achievement would you put on a highlight reel for yourself?
What proud moment do you hope defines how future generations remember you?
Is there an accomplishment you're proud of that came at a personal cost — would you do it again?
What do you want people to feel when they hear about the things you're most proud of?
